ux_issues.description: Shared trip view returned trip title but the itinerary items count could not be parsed from the shared endpoint response (response structure may differ from expected).
ux_issues.suggestion: Ensure /shared/{token} returns itinerary items in a predictable structure.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI re-asked about sesame allergy after user already answered 'no' in the previous message
ai_behavior_issues.context: In step 3b (safety preparation), the AI asked again 'Do you also need to avoid sesame?' despite Priya answering 'sesame is fine, I do NOT have a sesame allergy' in the previous message. Required a firm correction in step 3c.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Step 3b response was thin — AI claimed it had 'already shared' EpiPen and phrase info but had not done so in the current message
ai_behavior_issues.context: When asked specifically about EpiPen rules and Japanese allergen words, the AI gave a brief response referencing earlier information it had supposedly shared, rather than providing the detailed answer. Required a follow-up message to get the full information.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated strong understanding of compound dietary restrictions in Japan. It correctly identified soy sauce containing wheat, dashi being fish-based, miso potentially containing barley, and tempura being wheat-based. It recommended real restaurants (Ain Soph Journey, T's TanTan, Gluten Free Cafe Little Bird, Veg Out in Kyoto). Critically, the AI correctly ASKED about sesame sensitivity rather than fabricating an allergy — passing the sesame over-conflation check. The Yakkan Shoumei (import certificate for EpiPens) was mentioned proactively. Expert handoff to a Japan food specialist (Kenji) happened automatically and maintained full dietary context. The main weakness was context retention across turns — the AI re-asked the sesame question after it had been answered, and one safety-focused response was thin. Companion mode was appropriately concise and maintained all three restrictions. Overall, genuinely useful for a traveler with severe compound dietary restrictions.
